sources.population: Statista / World Population Review 2022. Lagos metropolitan population ~15.4 million. UN World Population Prospects 2024 Revision.
sources.sex_ratio: StatisticsTimes 2024 citing UN data. Nigeria sex ratio 102.3 males per 100 females → female proportion 49.4%. Applied to Lagos urban population.
sources.ethnicity_Lagos: World Population Review 2026. Lagos ethnic composition: Yoruba dominant (~60-65% Lagos State), Igbo (~15%), Hausa-Fulani (~10%), other groups ~15%. Engine maps all to 'black' (sub-Saharan African).
sources.ethnicity_Nigeria: World Population Review 2026. Nigeria national: Hausa-Fulani 30%, Yoruba 15.5%, Igbo 15.2%, other 39.3%. CIA World Factbook 2024.
sources.age_distribution: UN World Population Prospects 2024 Revision. Nigeria median age 18.3 years (very young population). ICU cohort age bands per Promise Abegunde spec: 55% age 65+, 45% age 18-64.
sources.comorbidity_DM: International Diabetes Federation (IDF) Diabetes Atlas 2021. Nigeria DM prevalence ~5.3% adults. Lagos urban higher ~7-9%.
sources.comorbidity_HTN: Adeloye D et al. Prevalence and awareness of hypertension in Nigeria. J Hypertens. 2020. Urban Nigeria HTN ~30-35%.
sources.sepsis_ICU: Murthy S et al. ICU capacity in low-income countries. PLoS One. 2015. Nigeria ICU beds ~0.07 per 100,000. Pneumonia leading sepsis source in sub-Saharan Africa.
sources.sepsis_vitals: Singer M et al. Sepsis-3. JAMA. 2016;315(8):801-810.
sources.afebrile_presentation: Gavazzi G, Krause KH. Ageing and infection. Lancet Infect Dis. 2002;2(11):659-666.
sources.infection_source: Rhee C et al. JAMA Intern Med. 2017;177(7):944-951. Pneumonia upweighted for sub-Saharan Africa urban context per WHO Africa region data.

ethnicity_label_note: Engine maps to 'black' for sub-Saharan African populations. Lagos is predominantly Yoruba (~60%), Igbo (~15%), Hausa (~10%), other Nigerian groups (~15%). No subgroup-specific clinical parameters available.
calibration_notes: Ethnicity mapped to 'black' — engine has no Yoruba/Igbo/Hausa subgroup clinical parameters. No published Lagos-specific subgroup sepsis data found.
DM prevalence in urban Lagos (~7-9%) higher than US baseline. Engine does not yet apply region-specific comorbidity prevalence adjustments — documented gap.
HTN prevalence urban Nigeria ~30-35% (Adeloye 2020) — lower than US baseline used in engine. Documented gap.
Afebrile and AMS-only presentation rates calibrated to age/comorbidity only — no Lagos-specific sepsis presentation data in published literature.
Pneumonia upweighted as primary infection source consistent with sub-Saharan Africa ICU sepsis epidemiology.
Time-to-deterioration fields modeled only — no Lagos ICU-specific calibration data available.
ICU infrastructure in Lagos is severely limited (Murthy 2015) — cohort represents a modeled ICU population, not a population-representative sample.

continuous_vars._source: IDF Diabetes Atlas 2021; Adeloye 2020 Nigeria HTN data; WHO Africa region NCD surveillance; NHANES reference for lab norms.

hba1c.source: IDF Atlas 2021; urban Lagos DM prevalence ~7-9%; lower mean than US due to lower overall DM burden

bmi.source: WHO Africa NCD surveillance; lower mean BMI in sub-Saharan Africa vs US/European populations

systolic_bp.source: Adeloye 2020; urban Nigeria HTN ~30-35% prevalence; higher systolic in ICU-presenting patients

creatinine.source: NHANES reference adjusted for Africa region; limited access to dialysis drives higher observed creatinine at ICU presentation; KDIGO 2022

egfr.source: CKD-EPI 2021; ICU-presenting patients in resource-limited settings have higher CKD burden at presentation

temporal_trends._source: IDF Diabetes Atlas 2021-2030 projections; WHO AFR NCD trend data; UN Population Prospects 2024

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 6-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 6-month drift interval

6.source: IDF Atlas 2021; WHO AFR NCD trends; UN Population Prospects 2024

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 12-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 12-month drift interval

12.source: IDF Atlas 2021; WHO AFR NCD trends; UN Population Prospects 2024

disease_prevalence_shift._unit: absolute prevalence change (fraction) at 18-month drift interval

prescription_shift._unit: relative change fraction at 18-month drift interval

18.source: IDF Atlas 2021; WHO AFR NCD trends; UN Population Prospects 2024
